Footer.tagline: Camping in the heart of Mors

Home.heroTitle: Wake up to nature on Mors
Home.heroSubtitle: Authentic camping in Sejerslev — surrounded by the Limfjord, Moler cliffs, and wide open skies
Home.introText: Nestled in the quiet village of Sejerslev on the island of Mors, our campsite offers a genuine escape into Danish nature. Whether you come for a weekend or a full holiday, you'll find space to breathe, explore, and truly relax.
Home.introText2: Mors is the largest island in the Limfjord — known for dramatic Moler cliffs, peaceful beaches, charming villages, and some of Denmark's most unspoiled landscapes.
Home.feature1Description: Campsites, motorhome pitches, cozy cottages, camping fields, and seasonal spots — find the perfect way to stay.
Home.feature2Description: No noise, no stress — just birdsong, campfire cosiness, and time to be together. Here, nature sets the pace.
Home.feature3Description: Minutes from Hanklit's 55-million-year-old cliffs, Skaerbaek Cliff, and the peaceful shores of the Limfjord.
Home.feature4Description: Dogs are welcome and stay for free. We have a dedicated dog run so your four-legged friend can enjoy the holiday too.
Home.altSection1Title: The perfect base for exploring Mors
Home.altSection1Text: From our campsite, you're just a short drive or bike ride from everything the island has to offer — Skaargaard, Fossil and Mill Museum, Nykobing Mors, Museum Mors, and the impressive cliffs at Hanklit and Skaerbaek.
Home.altSection2Text: There's something special about mornings with birdsong, evenings around the campfire, and days spent without a schedule. At Camping Slap Af, we believe the best holidays are the simple ones.
Home.testimonial1Text: We come back every summer. The kids love the playground and we love the peace and quiet. It feels like a second home.
Home.testimonial2Text: Beautiful location, very clean facilities, and the friendliest hosts. Mors is a hidden gem and this campsite is the perfect way to experience it.
Home.testimonial3Text: We discovered Mors by accident and Camping Slap Af made it unforgettable. Can't wait to return with the grandchildren.
Home.ctaDescription: Book your spot at Camping Slap Af and experience the best of Mors. Season open until October 18, 2026.

About.title: About Camping Slap Af
About.subtitle: A place where nature sets the pace
About.storyText1: Camping Slap Af is located in the charming village of Sejerslev on the island of Mors in the Limfjord. We offer an authentic and relaxed camping experience surrounded by extraordinary nature.
About.storyText2: Our campsite is the perfect base for exploring Mors' many attractions — from the impressive Moler cliffs to the cozy villages and rich wildlife.
About.locationText1: Mors is the largest island in the Limfjord and offers a varied landscape with dramatic cliffs, peaceful beaches and green hills. Sejerslev is located in the northwestern part of the island, close to Hanklit and the world-famous fossils.
About.locationText2: The area is a paradise for nature lovers, hikers, and cyclists. With a rich cultural history and warm local community, Mors offers something for everyone.
About.value1Description: We take care of the nature around us and encourage our guests to enjoy it with respect.
About.value2Description: We believe camping is about togetherness — with family, with friends and with new acquaintances.
About.value3Description: No unnecessary distractions. Just you, nature and a great experience.

Accommodation.title: Accommodation
Accommodation.subtitle: Find the perfect accommodation for your stay at Camping Slap Af
Accommodation.campsiteDescription: Spacious campsites with soft grass and good distance to neighbours. Electricity available. All spots have the same price regardless of size.
Accommodation.camperDescription: Level motorhome pitches with electrical connection and water access nearby. Suitable for motorhomes and caravans of all sizes.
Accommodation.cottageDescription: Cozy cottage for 4 people with basic facilities. Single flat rate regardless of number of guests.
Accommodation.fieldDescription: Open camping field with space for tents and smaller camping equipment. Great for groups and scouts.
Accommodation.seasonalDescription: Seasonal pitch for the full season. Electricity billed by consumption (4 DKK/kWh). Perfect if you want your own fixed spot all summer.
Accommodation.priceBasis: All prices are based on 2 adults. For 1 person, 20 DKK is deducted per day.
Accommodation.cottageFlat: Flat rate regardless of number of guests
Accommodation.facilitiesDescription: All guests have access to our shared facilities.
Accommodation.dogFriendlyText: Dogs are welcome and stay for free. They must be kept on a leash on site. Maximum 3 dogs (more by arrangement). We have a dedicated dog run.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.comingSoonText: We are working on an online booking system so you can soon reserve your spot directly here on the website.
Booking.contactCta: In the meantime, feel free to contact us to reserve your spot.

Activities.title: Activities
Activities.subtitle: There is plenty to experience — both on site and in the surrounding area
Activities.activity1Description: Large playground for the little ones with room to play and explore.
Activities.activity2Description: Pick fresh herbs and vegetables from our shared vegetable garden.
Activities.activity3Description: Gather the family around the fire pit in the evening. We provide the firewood.
Activities.activity4Description: Fenced dog run where your dog can run free and play off-leash.
Activities.nearby1Description: Impressive Moler cliffs with fossils from 55 million years ago. Just a few kilometres from the site.
Activities.nearby2Description: Dramatic coastal cliff with views over the Limfjord and fascinating geological layers.
Activities.nearby3Description: Visit the beautiful Skaargaard and experience the history and culture of Mors up close.
Activities.nearby4Description: Explore Mors' unique fossils and historic mills at this fascinating museum.
Activities.nearby5Description: Experience the historic mills and learn about their role in Mors' agricultural history.
Activities.nearby6Description: The island's charming main town with shops, restaurants, harbour, and cultural life.
Activities.nearby7Description: Dive into Mors' fascinating natural and cultural history at this well-maintained museum.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.subtitle: We'd love to hear from you
Contact.hoursText: Reception is open daily during the camping season (until October 18, 2026). Contact us for specific hours.
Contact.mapDescription: We are located in Sejerslev on the northwestern tip of Mors, close to Hanklit.
Contact.formComingSoon: Contact form coming soon. Please use email or phone in the meantime.
